text.title: Vector DB Security — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: no anonymous access, TLS everywhere, private network only, audit every admin call, PII handling procedure.

rule.statement: Production DB MUST require authentication (API key OR OIDC). Anonymous access endpoint disabled.
rule.rationale: Default-open vector DBs are scanned + exploited within hours of public exposure. Auth is the first wall.

rule.statement: All listeners MUST use TLS ≥1.2. Plain HTTP listeners are forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Embedded queries can contain sensitive payloads (PII, business secrets). Plain-text transport leaks both queries and results.

rule.statement: DB listener MUST bind to a private subnet OR be reachable only via a VPC-internal LB. Public-internet exposure is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Even with auth, exposed listener invites enumeration + DDoS. Network isolation is defence in depth.

rule.statement: Every admin operation (collection create / drop / config change, key rotate, schema change) MUST emit an audit log entry with timestamp + actor + action + outcome.
rule.rationale: Forensic ability post-incident requires reconstruction of admin actions. Silent admin changes block forensics.

rule.statement: If corpus may contain PII, security-config MUST declare: detection method (Presidio / regex), redaction or pseudonymisation, GDPR right-to-erasure response time, retention period.
rule.rationale: PII without process is a compliance violation. Vector DBs are especially tricky: erasure requires removing the vector AND the original.
